paragraphs.summary: This supervisory statement is addressed to UK Solvency II firms and to Lloyd’s. It sets out the Prudential Regulation Authority’s (PRA’s) approach to implementing the Solvency II Directive requirement
In particular, this statement explains requirements regarding the following topics:

This statement expands on the PRA’s general approach as set out in its insurance approach document.1 By clearly and consistently explaining its expectations of firms in relation to the particular area
This statement has been subject to public consultation2 and reflects the feedback that was received by the PRA.

sections.chapter_title: Pursuit of life and non-life insurance activity

paragraphs.summary: Under section 19 of the Financial Services and Markets Act 2000 (FSMA), a firm may not carry on a regulated activity unless it has permission to do so (or is exempt in relation to the particular activ

A firm’s permission can be varied so as to add other classes. The permission of an existing composite firm may be varied by adding classes of both general insurance business and long-term insurance bu

It is PRA policy not to grant or vary permission if that would allow a newly established firm, or an existing firm engaging solely in general insurance business or solely in long-term insurance busine

sections.chapter_title: Separation of life and non-life insurance management

paragraphs.summary: For the purpose of Composites 2.2, the PRA would expect a composite firm to:
The firm should consider the appropriateness of the apportionment referred to in Composites 3.3(2), having regard to the requirements in Composites 2.2.

For the purpose of Composites 4.2, firms should note that the Solvency II Regulations set out the method for calculating the notional minimum capital requirement (MCR).
